technique: URL-encoded newline with $() subshell — WAF regex may match literal chars but miss encoded form
IFS abuse without special shell metachar — semicolon is in block list but IFS reassignment may slip through if regex is non-greedy
Unicode non-breaking space substitution for spaces; backtick is blocked but testing if unicode spaces cause regex miss on backtick pattern
Scheme is https (whitelisted), host is 127.0.0.1 (whitelisted) — WAF allows both; tests if port 8080 is accessible
localhost is whitelisted host and https is whitelisted scheme — attempt to reach internal file path via HTTP
Credential-injection URL confusion: 127.0.0.1 is in the username portion, may pass host whitelist check on extracted userinfo rather than hostname
